briefdescription.para: Which I2C hop failed the last reachability probe (feeds node health reporting, #163).

memberdef.argsstring: (U8G2 &oled, const DrumReadout &readout, DrumFont font=DrumFont::LARGE, float xOffsetMm=0.0f, float yOffsetMm=0.0f)
memberdef.name: DrumDisplay

briefdescription.para: Construct and register a direct-bus drum display.

parameterdescription.para: Caller-owned U8G2 (already begin()'d, rotation set). Must outlive this.
Descriptor for this readout (sources, geometry, flag). Must outlive this.
X shift (mm) of the whole digit block, registers it to the faceplate window.
Y shift (mm) of the digit block centre line.

simplesect.para: The sketch owns Wire.begin() + oled.begin(). Geometry is auto-fitted in configure().

memberdef.argsstring: (U8G2 &oled, const DrumReadout &readout, I2cMux &mux, uint8_t channel, DrumFont font=DrumFont::LARGE, float xOffsetMm=0.0f, float yOffsetMm=0.0f)
memberdef.name: DrumDisplay

briefdescription.para: Construct and register a muxed drum display (one TCA9548A branch).

parameterdescription.para: Shared TCA9548A selector. Must outlive this.
TCA9548A channel 0–7 this panel sits on.

simplesect.para: The class calls mux.select(channel) before geometry fit in configure() and before each sendBuffer() so interleaved displays never write to the wrong panel.

briefdescription.para: Compute pixel geometry from the panel + descriptor, set the font, blank the panel.

simplesect.para: Called once by PanelGroup::setup() after chip init. Selects the mux channel (if any) first, reads getDisplayWidth()/Height(), runs the geometry fit, and clears the OLED. The sketch owns oled.begin(); this does NOT call begin().

briefdescription.para: Decode one CTRL_BCAST packet into this readout's digits/flag. Never draws.

parameterdescription.para: DCS-BIOS output address from the packet. Ignored unless it matches a DrumSource.address or the flag.address of this readout.

simplesect.para: Cheap: decodes value→digit(s), splices into the target number, sets the dirty flag. The expensive full-buffer I2C send happens in update(), never here.

briefdescription.para: Advance the ease/snap animation and push one frame if needed.

simplesect.para: Called every loop(). ~60 fps frame gate; early-out when idle (settled AND not dirty). On a live frame: mux.select(channel), ease each place toward target/10^place with SNAP_SETTLE jump handling, render every cell with setClipWindow, then one sendBuffer().

briefdescription.para: Change glyph size at runtime (e.g. swap a cramped 6-digit readout to SMALL).

parameterdescription.para: New DrumFont. Re-fits geometry on the next update() so cells re-auto-fit.

briefdescription.para: Re-register the digit block to the faceplate window at runtime.

parameterdescription.para: New X shift (mm) of the digit block — measure the cutout misalignment after assembly.
New Y shift (mm, centre line) of the digit block.

simplesect.para: Marks geometry dirty; the new offsets apply on the next rendered frame. Resolution- independent (converted to px via the panel scale), so the same mm value registers correctly on any OLED size.
Rounds to whole pixels, so the smallest useful step is ~0.25 mm (≈1 px); a 0.1 mm nudge is sub-pixel and may not move. The 1.3" 128x64 (~0.23 mm/px) is the coarser panel.

briefdescription.para: Contract: probe this device's reachability (e.g. the mux ACKs and the device ACKs).

simplesect.para: true if reachable. The implementer records any fault detail it wants to report.
Must be cheap — a single address probe, no payload — and must not throw or block beyond one bounded I2C transaction.

briefdescription.para: Rolling-drum OLED readout. One instance == one OLED panel.

detaileddescription.para: Construct at global scope so OutputBase self-registers it. onControlPacket() only decodes + flags dirty (cheap); update() does the ~60 fps gate, channel reselect, ease+snap, render, and the single expensive sendBuffer().
